codeReview.summary: The feature materially improves parser-built PendingDocument layer/entity provenance and adds focused regressions, but commit bc4eba8 still left a blocking correctness gap: entity layer relationships were only preserved when encoded inline as L<name>, not when expressed via layer handles/references, so the outward parser-facing layer provenance contract was not fully met by this feature commit.

issues.description: semantic_entity() in commit bc4eba8 did not accept the layer handle map and only derived layer_name from inline L<name> fragments, defaulting to an empty string otherwise. Entities whose DWG semantic record referenced a layer by handle/reference therefore lost their outward-facing layer provenance on PendingDocument.entities, violating the feature requirement that parsed layer references become parser-visible provenance rather than needing resolver-only/manual reconstruction.

summary: Reviewed mission docs, transcript skeleton, handoff, skill file, and commit bc4eba8 for dwg-parser-pending-layer-entity-semantics-contract. Result: fail, because the feature commit exposed parsed layers and owner provenance but still dropped handle/reference-based entity layer provenance until a later follow-up fix.
